name: lead-readiness-probe-before-dispatch
description: Before writing specs and spawning builders, run the dispatch's own acceptance gates against the current branch state. If the gates already pass, the work is already done — escalate to coordinator with status mail rather than spawning a builder. Cheap probe (one-shot rg + git log main..HEAD + read the ADR's six required edits) catches duplicate-dispatch scenarios in seconds.

Seeds (.seeds/issues.jsonl) is git-tracked, so issues created on a feature branch are stranded there — the backlog must live on main. Discovered when 21 backlog items vanished after branching to wave/0.
resolution: Create Seeds issues only while on main; if stranded, recover with 'git show <branch>:.seeds/issues.jsonl' then 'git checkout <branch> -- .seeds/issues.jsonl' onto main.

description: Cannot cleanly integrate wave PRs while main CI is RED: every PR inherits the same pre-existing failures, so you can't distinguish 'my change broke it' from 'already broken'. agent-manager main CI was red on flaky/env-coupled tests (bun-not-in-PATH community tests, 8s age-secrets timing, apply dry-run JSON EOF, init-project isolation leak) while local bun test passed 3064/0.
resolution: Fix CI-green FIRST (its own branch/PR) to establish a true baseline, then cascade-merge the rest of the stack against green.

content: Partition parallel wave branches by DISJOINT file ownership so they are conflict-free by construction. The one shared hub file (src/core/controller.ts — edited by both the apply-notice wave and the secrets-decode wave) forces merge-order = wave-order, later wave rebases. See docs/audit/assessment-2026-05-31/INTEGRATION-PLAN.md.
